Title: Lysine Matters: Genetic and Biotechnological Innovations to Combat Protein Malnutrition

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppression or knockout reduces lysine catabolism and increases seed lysine accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LKR/SDH is consistently summarized as a major lysine catabolic enzyme limiting lysine accumulation in seeds. 2. RNAi, knockdown, or CRISPR/Cas suppression of LKR/SDH in rice and maize increased free or total lysine, often strongly when combined with enhanced biosynthesis. 3. Seed-targeted suppression is emphasized as more effective than whole-plant effects for biofortification.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of transgenic knockdown, RNAi silencing, CRISPR/Cas9 editing, metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible metabolic disruption; uncertain long-term effects of blocking major amino acid catabolism; may affect seed physiology if not spatially targeted
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to lysine biofortification, the review notes that high lysine in leaves does not necessarily translate to high seed lysine, implying transport limitations and the need for seed-specific targeting.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Lysine-histidine transporter family; integral membrane amino acid transporter
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely influences lysine uptake and source-to-sink allocation rather than biosynthesis itself
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LHT1 is summarized as a transporter relevant to amino acid uptake and internal distribution. 2. Loss-of-function in rice or Arabidopsis causes impaired amino acid homeostasis, growth defects, and low yield. 3. The review proposes LHT manipulation as a future route to improve lysine loading into seeds.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of mutant analysis and expression studies from cited literature
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Stunted growth, reduced biomass, low yield when transporter function is impaired
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as a candidate common gene for improving source-to-sink lysine transport; exact crop isoform roles remain unclear.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Lysine-histidine transporter family; integral membrane amino acid transporter
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: May affect lysine transport but has pleiotropic links to ACC transport and ethylene-related processes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LHT2 is discussed as an amino acid transporter with possible relevance to lysine partitioning. 2. Arabidopsis overexpression altered ACC sensitivity, suggesting broader transport/signaling roles. 3. The review highlights pleiotropy as a challenge for using LHTs in biofortification.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of overexpression phenotypes from cited study
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible unintended effects on ethylene biosynthesis/signaling and plant development
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the review treats LHTs as candidate targets for lysine biofortification through transport engineering.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Endosperm regulatory locus affecting storage protein composition
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation increases kernel lysine and tryptophan by altering storage protein composition; foundational for QPM breeding
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. o2 is repeatedly summarized as a classic high-lysine maize allele used in conventional breeding and MAS. 2. The mutation improves amino acid profile but initially causes soft endosperm and poor agronomic performance. 3. Modifier-based breeding restored kernel hardness in Quality Protein Maize while retaining enhanced lysine.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of mutant breeding, backcrossing, MAS, and phenotype/metabolite analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Soft or opaque endosperm; poor kernel texture; initial yield/agronomic penalties
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although o2 encodes a transcription factor in maize literature, in this review it is mainly treated within breeding summaries; because the paper broadly summarizes it as a core biofortification locus affecting storage protein composition, it is included.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introgression with o2 improves lysine content in maize breeding programs
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. o16 is summarized as a major high-lysine maize locus used in MAS and gene pyramiding. 2. Combining o16 with o2 enhanced lysine while maintaining agronomic traits in elite lines. 3. It is a practical breeding target rather than a mechanistically detailed metabolic gene in this review.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of marker-assisted backcrossing and field trait evaluation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: NA
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Molecular identity/function is not detailed in the review.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts indirectly; combined with o2/o16 can enhance lysine and tryptophan during maize kernel development
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. wx1 is described as a starch biosynthesis gene whose mutation interacts with lysine-related loci. 2. Double mutant wx1/o2 showed synergistic increases in lysine and tryptophan. 3. It illustrates metabolic crosstalk between starch and amino acid metabolism.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of mutant combination and grain composition analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: NA
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a common gene because it affects lysine indirectly through kernel metabolic context rather than a direct lysine biosynthetic/catabolic step.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases seed lysine and protein content
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Potato SBgLR overexpression in maize increased lysine and protein content. 2. Co-expression with TSRF1 also improved salt tolerance. 3. Illustrates storage-protein engineering as a safe sequestration strategy for lysine enhancement.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of transgenic overexpression and seed composition analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: NA
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts through storage/sequestration rather than direct pathway catalysis.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Seed-specific expression increases total lysine and total protein in maize seeds
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. sb401 expression is summarized as a successful lysine-rich protein strategy in maize. 2. It increased total lysine and protein over multiple generations. 3. Effects were seed-focused without reported adverse effects on vegetative tissues.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of seed-specific transgenic overexpression and compositional analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: NA

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression elevates maize lysine without obvious agronomic penalties
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cotton GhLRP overexpression in maize increased lysine substantially. 2. Nutritional gains were stably inherited. 3. Agronomic and quality traits were reportedly unaffected.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of transgenic overexpression and multigenerational trait analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious adverse agronomic or quality effects reported

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Seed-specific overexpression increases total grain lysine in rice
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RLRH1 is summarized as a lysine-rich histone protein used to raise protein-bound lysine in rice grain. 2. Together with RLRH2 it achieved about 35% lysine increase. 3. Grain chalkiness was observed in transgenic seeds.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of seed-specific overexpression and grain amino acid analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Seed chalkiness correlated with protein and lysine accumulation

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Seed-specific overexpression increases total grain lysine in rice
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RLRH2 is paired with RLRH1 as a storage-protein strategy for lysine enhancement in rice. 2. Increased total lysine while maintaining amino acid balance. 3. Some chalkiness occurred in transgenic seeds.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of seed-specific overexpression and grain amino acid analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Seed chalkiness

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression in maize boosts lysine and protein content and promotes protein body formation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis MAP18 is summarized as enhancing maize seed lysine and protein content. 2. It promoted protein body formation in endosperm. 3. Nutritional gains persisted in hybrid progeny without agronomic penalty.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of transgenic expression and seed phenotype analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No compromise of agronomic performance reported

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Endosperm-specific expression increases total lysine in rice
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Psophocarpus tetragonolobus LRP expression increased rice grain lysine and other amino acids. 2. Stable inheritance was reported over generations. 3. Some lines showed undesirable grain phenotypes.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of endosperm-specific transgenic expression and grain composition analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Unfavourable grain phenotypes in some lines

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Synthetic protein expression increases lysine, threonine, total amino acids, and crude protein in rice
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TKTKK1 is a synthetic lysine-rich protein gene used for rice biofortification. 2. It increased lysine and multiple amino acid/protein traits. 3. Agronomic effects were limited according to the review.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of transgenic expression and compositional analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Limited effect on agronomic traits reported

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Synthetic protein expression increases lysine, threonine, total amino acids, and crude protein in rice
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TKTKK2 is summarized alongside TKTKK1 as a synthetic lysine-rich protein engineering strategy. 2. It improved lysine-related nutritional traits in rice. 3. The review notes limited agronomic disturbance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of transgenic expression and compositional analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Limited effect on agronomic traits reported

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Seed albumin / protein-quality improving storage protein
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression improves total protein and lysine in rice while maintaining seed viability and yield
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AmA1 is summarized as a storage-protein engineering target improving rice seed protein quality. 2. It increased total protein and lysine. 3. Seed viability and yield were maintained.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of transgenic overexpression and agronomic evaluation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major yield or viability penalties reported

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing or editing raises lysine by reducing lysine-poor storage proteins while maintaining kernel texture in some designs
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 19-kDa α-zeins are major low-lysine storage proteins targeted by RNAi or CRISPR/Cas9. 2. Partial editing increased lysine without needing o2 and preserved vitreous endosperm. 3. Combined suppression with metabolic engineering further improved total lysine.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of RNAi silencing, CRISPR/Cas9 editing, and seed composition analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Needs careful balancing to avoid seed quality defects

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing increases kernel lysine and can maintain kernel texture
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 22-kDa α-zeins were co-silenced with 19-kDa α-zeins to increase lysine. 2. This changed storage protein composition toward higher lysine. 3. Kernel texture could be maintained in optimized lines.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of RNAi silencing and compositional analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible nitrogen partitioning imbalance if excessively altered

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Maize storage protein involved in protein body formation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced expression indirectly improves essential amino acid ratio including lysine while retaining vitreous endosperm
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 27-kDa γ-zein is summarized as a key storage protein controlled by ZmbZIP22. 2. Reduced expression increased the ratio of non-zein proteins and enhanced lysine. 3. Endosperm vitreousness was retained.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of CRISPR/Cas9 mutant analysis and storage protein profiling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: NA

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CRISPR editing reduces α-kafirin and increases lysine and protein digestibility in sorghum
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. K1C family editing is summarized as an effective CRISPR strategy in sorghum. 2. Reduced α-kafirin improved digestibility and increased lysine. 3. Nutritional effects were observed in T1 and T2 seeds.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of CRISPR/Cas9 editing and seed quality assays
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: NA

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CRISPR editing reduces γ-kafirin, improving lysine, digestibility, and growth vigor
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. K2G editing in sorghum reduced γ-kafirin. 2. Digestibility increased strongly and lysine deficiency was alleviated. 3. Mutant plants also showed improved growth vigor.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of CRISPR/Cas9 editing and protein digestibility analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: NA

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Common aspartate-family entry step feeding lysine/threonine/methionine/isoleucine branches
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Phosphorylates aspartate to aspartyl-phosphate at the entry point of the aspartate pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor flux toward lysine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Feedback-insensitive AK variants promote increased lysine accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AK is repeatedly summarized as a major regulatory bottleneck in the aspartate pathway. 2. Feedback-insensitive native or microbial AK variants increase free lysine in multiple crops. 3. Combining deregulated AK with DHDPS and/or LKR/SDH suppression is presented as a leading lysine biofortification strategy.
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Escherichia coli; Corynebacterium glutamicum; Corynebacterium pekinense
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Oryza sativa; Nicotiana tabacum; Glycine max
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of overexpression, mutagenesis, structural studies, enzyme assays, metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Abiotic stress discussed as influencing pathway regulation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive deregulation can cause pleiotropy, developmental abnormalities, and yield penalties if not spatially controlled
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for base editing, promoter editing, or transgenic expression of feedback-insensitive alleles, especially with seed-specific promoters and stacking with DHDPS/LKR-SDH interventions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review emphasizes AK as one of the top enzyme targets for precise genome editing to relieve feedback inhibition.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses aspartate semialdehyde with pyruvate to form 2,3-dihydrodipicolinate in the committed step to lysine
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed step; branch point into lysine synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Committed-step enzyme and major feedback-controlled bottleneck
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly commits metabolic flux toward lysine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Feedback-insensitive DHDPS variants markedly enhance lysine accumulation in seeds and other tissues
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DHDPS is highlighted throughout the review as the committed and most widely engineered enzyme for lysine biofortification. 2. Feedback-insensitive variants from plants or bacteria increase lysine in rice, soybean, canola, and false flax. 3. Excessive lysine can trigger seed defects or yield penalties in some systems, making tissue-specific expression important.
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Vitis vinifera; Corynebacterium glutamicum; Escherichia coli; Camelina sativa
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Rice; Soybean; Canola; False flax; plants
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Oryza sativa; Glycine max; Brassica napus; Camelina sativa
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Seed-specific promoters; glutelinD1; phaseolin; constitutive CaMV35S
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of structural studies, mutagenesis, transgenic overexpression, metabolite profiling, agronomic evaluation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excessive accumulation can cause wrinkled seeds, poor germination, dark-brown endosperm, or reduced seed yield depending on context
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Premier target for feedback-insensitive engineering, prime/base editing, promoter tuning, and stacking with AK and LKR/SDH suppression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review repeatedly proposes DHDPS editing as central to future non-transgenic high-lysine cereals.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Competing homoserine branch toward threonine and methionine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Bifunctional enzyme contributing to aspartate pathway control and flux toward threonine/methionine branch
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as lysine committed step, but key competing-branch control node
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with lysine branch and diverts precursor flux away from lysine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of AK-HSDH2 decreases flux to competing branches and raises lysine abundance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AK-HSDH2 is discussed as part of the competing homoserine pathway. 2. Arabidopsis mutants lacking AK-HSDH2 showed decreased threonine/methionine flux and increased lysine. 3. The review proposes suppressing this competing branch as one route to redirect flux toward lysine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of knockout mutant metabolic phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May perturb amino acid balance across threonine/methionine/isoleucine branches
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the review explicitly frames HSD suppression as a lysine-enhancing engineering target.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Storage protein-associated lysine enrichment and stress response
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Co-expression with SBgLR increased maize lysine and improved salt tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tomato TSRF1 is summarized as a regulatory gene used alongside SBgLR in maize engineering. 2. Co-expression improved lysine and protein content. 3. It also conferred improved salt tolerance, indicating dual nutritional and stress-related roles.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of transgenic co-expression and compositional/stress phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful in stacked engineering where nutritional and stress-tolerance traits are jointly targeted
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review does not detail direct downstream lysine-pathway targets.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Storage protein composition affecting lysine proportion
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates or maintains expression of 27-kDa γ-zein
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conditionally important controller of lysine proportion through storage protein rebalancing
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation increases non-zein proportion and raises lysine, tryptophan, and methionine while retaining vitreous endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmbZIP22 was identified as a key regulator of 27-kDa γ-zein in maize. 2. CRISPR/Cas9 zmbzip22 mutants reduced γ-zein expression and increased the proportion of non-zein proteins. 3. This rebalanced seed protein composition and improved essential amino acid content including lysine.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of CRISPR/Cas9 mutant analysis, expression analysis, storage protein profiling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising non-transgenic target for improving amino acid balance without using the pleiotropic o2 mutation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the clearest regulatory-gene examples in the review directly linked to lysine improvement.
